notes: The spec's Expected: bullets are 'Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch' and 'PID bound within 1s' — both satisfied by the pre-session row (proc-<PID>). The recipe sends a 1-token nudge (lazy-transcript workaround for claudecode) which produces a canonical ready → working → ready arc on the UUID-keyed session; the extra phases below assert that arc so the panel matches what the operator sees in the recording. Adding these is spec-faithful — they assert observable behavior the recipe definitively exercises — and tightens drift detection (a future daemon change that broke the cycle would fail validation here rather than slipping through).

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch
After the nudge prompt lands, the UUID-keyed session appears in ready — the proc-<PID> pre-session is handed off to the real session row
The nudge prompt drives ready → working on the UUID session
The single-token reply completes; state returns to ready
